The absolute, massive explosion of the Hansie Cronje match-fixing scandal completely consumed the Indian sporting and legal establishment. In a massive, highly anticipated, and deeply shocking development, the disgraced South African captain officially and aggressively provided a massive, formal confession to the King Commission in South Africa. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On June 28, 1914, an incredibly massive, absolutely catastrophic, and historically explosive assassination occurred in Sarajevo, Bosnia. Gavrilo Princip, a highly radicalized Yugoslav nationalist, brutally and aggressively assassinated Archduke Franz Ferdinand, the heir to the Austro-Hungarian Empire, and his wife Sophie. This incredibly massive, shocking event aggressively triggered a massive, unyielding web of mutual defense treaties across Europe, fundamentally acting as the absolute, direct catalyst that ignited the unimaginable, horrific devastation of World War I one month later.

On June 28, 1919, exactly five years after the assassination in Sarajevo, the incredibly massive, heavily punitive Treaty of Versailles was officially and aggressively signed in France. The massive diplomatic treaty officially and absolutely ended World War I. However, its incredibly harsh, massive economic reparations and strict territorial dismemberment aggressively imposed upon Germany deeply humiliated the nation. This massive, flawed treaty fundamentally seeded the absolute, deep-rooted German resentment that would aggressively fuel the rise of Adolf Hitler and World War II.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On June 28, 1921, P. V. Narasimha Rao, the massive, highly erudite, and incredibly pragmatic Indian politician, was officially born in Warangal (in present-day Telangana). Achieving massive historical immortality as the 9th Prime Minister of India, he aggressively took office during a massive, absolutely catastrophic balance-of-payments crisis. He heavily and aggressively initiated the monumental 1991 economic liberalization policies, aggressively dismantling the "License Raj" and fundamentally, absolutely saving the Indian economy from total, catastrophic collapse.

On June 28, 1972, Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis, the absolute, incredibly brilliant, and foundational Indian scientist and applied statistician, passed away in Calcutta. Universally revered for the massive "Mahalanobis distance," he aggressively founded the incredibly prestigious Indian Statistical Institute (ISI). His massive, unparalleled intellect was heavily utilized by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru to fundamentally design the massive, heavily industrialized strategy of the Second Five-Year Plan, cementing his massive legacy as the chief architect of Indian economic planning.
